name: 🇬🇧 DNC temporal-links
technique: Deterministic NxN temporal link matrix recording write order across memory locations, enabling forward/backward traversal of multi-step relational chains
metric: 98.8% avg accuracy on 7-step traversals vs 37% LSTM (Fig 2b), runner-reported from Nature 538
vetting: source+topic confirmed by reviewer; exact figure runner-extracted from paper. CROSS-BENCHMARK vs JEMHopQA-calibrated multihop (0.893) — treat as modeled evidence, confirm by porting temporal-links onto JEMHopQA.

name: 🇨🇭 generalized-KV memory
technique: Decouple key-value memory size from support-count via a redundancy parameter using outer-product superposition, deterministically compressing the store
metric: ~10x memory saving at 95% of original accuracy (r=10 vs mn=100), runner-reported from Sec IV-A/Fig 1
vetting: source+topic confirmed by reviewer; exact figure runner-extracted. NOTE: loop models the 10x footprint gain but NOT the ~5% accuracy cost — slightly favorable.

name: 🇨🇦 TARDIS wormholes
technique: Discrete tied read/write addressing storing selected hidden states into fixed cells, creating deterministic shortcut ('wormhole') edges that retain long-range dependencies
metric: 84.3% SNLI test acc vs 81.7% LSTM baseline (Table 4), runner-reported
maps_to: retain: shortcut edges between distant co-relevant facts
vetting: source+topic confirmed by reviewer; exact figure runner-extracted. CROSS-BENCHMARK (SNLI acc → retain recall) — modeled evidence; confirm by implementing shortcut-edges on the forgetting-pressure task.

name: 🇻🇳 UIT-ViQuAD XLM-R
technique: Learned XLM-R cross-lingual span retrieval on a native Vietnamese QA benchmark
vetting: REJECTED: (1) learned embedding front-end = the probabilistic layer Dilmun quarantines, not a deterministic additive primitive; (2) dominated by 🇮🇳 concept-id (crossling≈1.0), so on=0.87 cannot move the loop. Kept on record so the daily runner skips re-proposing Vietnam.

name: 🇫🇷 clique associative memory
technique: Sparse clustered associative memory (Gripon-Berrou): patterns stored as cliques across C-partite neuron clusters with LDPC-like sparse encoding; corrupted/partially-erased cues are recovered by a deterministic clique-finding retrieval rule (error-correcting recall)
benchmark: Sparse clustered network erased-cluster recall (Telecom Bretagne, France)
metric: 80% retrieval rate (clique-finding mcqd) vs ~20% standard sum-of-max, small scenario 5000 stored messages / 6 erased clusters, Figure 7; near-100% recovery in the 40k-message / 12-erased-cluster large scenario
maps_to: robustness: error-correcting recall of a stored pattern from a partially-erased cue
vetting: Runner-fetched Fig 7 number confirmed; lead mechanism-author V. Gripon at Telecom Bretagne, Brest, France (co-authors McGill/Canada, but GBNN is French-origin Gripon-Berrou). Deterministic clique retrieval, no learned embeddings. CROSS-BENCHMARK vs robustness (invariants+coverage sim) — modeled evidence; the 0.80 is the conservative small-scenario number (large-scenario approx 1.0). Confirm by porting clique-recall onto the forgetting-pressure/robustness task.

name: 🇦🇺 Tree-of-Time traversal
technique: Deterministic hierarchical temporal decomposition (Tree of Time) enforcing monotonic-timestamp constraints and multi-entity temporal synchronization during temporal-KG traversal, plus an explicit reusable experience-memory of verified reasoning traces
benchmark: MultiTQ temporal multi-hop QA (UNSW Sydney + CSIRO Data61, Australia)
metric: Hit@1 77.9% overall (GPT-4-Turbo backbone), Table 1; surpasses TempAgent baseline by 24.0 points
maps_to: compose/derive multi-hop traversal (+ retain via experience-memory reuse)
vetting: REJECTED by reviewer. Paper is real (arXiv 2510.13614, 'MemoTime', UNSW Sydney + CSIRO Data61, reviewer-fetched). But the 77.9% Hit@1 is produced by an LLM-orchestrated pipeline (Qwen3-4B/GPT-4-Turbo reference for question decomposition + answer generation) — the metric is NOT attributable to the deterministic Tree-of-Time traversal in isolation, so it violates the additive+deterministic rule (same class as the Vietnam learned-front-end rejection). Also dominated on multihop (0.779 < DNC 0.988 and the composed multihop stack). Kept on record so the daily runner skips re-proposing Australia/MemoTime.

name: 🇰🇷 memristor Hopfield recall
technique: Hardware-aware Hopfield associative memory whose energy landscape is reshaped by the intrinsic nonlinear I-V of a charge-trap memristor, giving deterministic superlinear pattern-storage capacity and energy-minimization recall/reconstruction of corrupted patterns
benchmark: 25x25 nonlinear memristor crossbar Hopfield capacity test (KIST, South Korea)
metric: empirical capacity scaling K ~ 0.3 x N^1.2 (superlinear) vs classical Hopfield K ~ 0.14N; reliable reconstruction of corrupted patterns (abstract + main text)
maps_to: robustness: error-correcting associative recall / consolidation of the store
vetting: REJECTED by reviewer on DATA-ONLY grounds. Paper is real (arXiv 2605.07223, 'A Hardware-aware Hopfield Network with a Nonlinear Memristor Array...', submitted 2026-05-08, reviewer-fetched; abstract confirms K~0.3 N^1.2). BUT the only cited number is a CAPACITY SCALING LAW, not a measured [0,1] robustness fraction — the ingested on=0.85 was an admitted MODELED PLACEHOLDER with no measured axis metric behind it, which the loop forbids (no candidate without a measured number on its axis). Deterministic mechanism is promising; re-admit ONLY after a real corrupted-pattern reconstruction accuracy in [0,1] is measured. Kept on record so the daily runner skips re-proposing this KIST paper until recalibrated.

name: 🇲🇽 entropic AMR recall
technique: Entropic Associative Memory: patterns stored in a discrete Associative Memory Register (AMR) via logical inclusive-disjunction; a partial/noisy cue is recovered by a deterministic entropy-based material-implication retrieval operation over the register table — error-correcting recall with no gradient training
benchmark: EMNIST-36 manuscript-symbol recall (Universidad de Guadalajara + UNAM, Mexico)
metric: recall 0.8361 (precision 0.8868) at 64x64 AMR using the full remembered corpus, Figure 8(f), runner-fetched via ar5iv
maps_to: robustness: error-correcting associative recall of a stored pattern from a partial/noisy cue
vetting: source+topic confirmed by reviewer fetch (Morales, Hernandez, Cruz, Cruz, Pineda; UdG/UNAM, Mexico). Deterministic register-based AMR (logical inclusive-disjunction store + material-implication/entropy retrieval), no learned embeddings — passes additive+deterministic. NOT dominated: robustness values are APPENDED to the robustness mean (not max'd), so this coexists with 🇫🇷 clique associative memory. CROSS-BENCHMARK: EMNIST symbol recall → Dilmun robustness (invariants+coverage sim) — modeled evidence, not proven; confirm by porting AMR entropic recall onto the forgetting-pressure/robustness task. on=0.8361 is the recall fraction (conservative vs precision 0.8868).

name: 🇫🇮 Voikko FST morphology
technique: Deterministic finite-state (two-level, Koskenniemi-style) Finnish morphological analyzer (Voikko / .vfst) mapping inflected surface word-forms to their canonical lemma + morphological analysis
benchmark: UD Finnish-TDT dev set (Voikko morphology; Finnish two-level FST tradition)
metric: 93.09% lemma accuracy at 99.35% coverage on UD Finnish-TDT dev, Table 4 / Sec 5.3, runner-fetched via ar5iv
maps_to: variant: normalize an inflected surface form to its canonical lemma
vetting: REJECTED (DOMINATED), not a data problem — the number is real and verified (deterministic Voikko .vfst FST; two-level Koskenniemi/Helsinki Finnish morphology). The specific paper is single-author 'Comonadic Morphophonology' (arXiv 2605.28484, author listed 'Independent Researcher'); nation attribution rests on the Finnish-origin mechanism, as with the 🇫🇷 GBNN case. REJECTED because the loop's variant axis is already saturated by 🇦🇪 root-key (sub_variant → 1.0) and variant candidates fold in via max(), so on=0.9309 < 1.0 cannot move the loop (same class as the 🇻🇳 Vietnam dominated rejection). Kept on record so the daily runner skips re-proposing Finnish/Voikko morphology.

name: 🇧🇷 Zadeh max-C PAFMM
technique: Zadeh max-C / min-D Projection Autoassociative Fuzzy Morphological Memory: one-step projection of a noise-corrupted input onto the family of max-C/min-D combinations of the stored items, with a noise-masking rule — deterministic, no floating-point operations, no training
benchmark: AR face database with noise+blur, standalone Resized+VZM (Univ. Campinas + UFRB, Brazil)
metric: ARR 0.9667 on AR face DB for the standalone deterministic 'Resized+VZM' memory (raw resized pixels → PAFMM), Table 5; runner-extracted. Learned ResNet+VZM rows EXCLUDED as not attributable to the deterministic memory.
maps_to: robustness: error-correcting recall of a stored pattern from a noise/blur-corrupted cue
vetting: Reviewer re-fetched via ar5iv: authors Santos (UFRB) + Valle (Unicamp), Brazil CONFIRMED, and the deterministic 'no floating-point operations' max-C/min-D projection mechanism CONFIRMED verbatim. The exact Table-5 cell (ARR 0.9667) is runner-reported — ar5iv truncated Sec 7 so the cell was not personally re-eyeballed (same runner-extracted standard as 🇬🇧 DNC / 🇨🇦 TARDIS). Correctly uses ONLY the standalone Resized+VZM row; ResNet+VZM (learned front-end) excluded — passes additive+deterministic. Robustness values APPEND to the robustness mean, so additive alongside 🇫🇷 clique (0.80) and 🇲🇽 EAM (0.8361); on=0.9667 is the new best single robustness primitive. CROSS-BENCHMARK: AR-face ARR → Dilmun robustness (invariants+coverage sim) — modeled, not proven; confirm by porting projection-recall onto the forgetting-pressure/robustness task.

name: 🇮🇹 PGM-index
technique: Piecewise Geometric Model index: recursive optimal piecewise-linear approximation over sorted keys (built by a linear-time convex-hull algorithm) mapping a key to a bounded-error position, corrected by a bounded binary search — a deterministic succinct index with worst-case guarantees
benchmark: Predecessor/range-query indexing up to 1B keys (Univ. Pisa; PVLDB 13(8))
metric: abstract/Sec.7: matches a static B+-tree/CSS-tree query performance within '83x less space' (static); dynamic setting up to 71% faster within 1140x less space. footprint_delta=1/83≈0.012 (conservative static figure). 83x now INDEPENDENTLY CONFIRMED across VLDB PDF + ACM DL (10.14778/3389133.3389135) + ResearchGate + the paper's own HN thread title.
maps_to: efficiency: footprint of the index over the stored memory
vetting: HELD PENDING (status!=vetted, so NOT loaded by the loop). UPDATE (this tick): blocker #1 (uncorroborated) is now RESOLVED — authors Ferragina & Vinciguerra (Univ. Pisa, PVLDB 13(8):1162-1175, 2020) and the '83x less space at B+-tree query performance' figure are independently confirmed across multiple sources. Deterministic PLA index (streaming convex-hull, exact error-corrected rank), not learned. REMAINING blocker before promotion is a JUDGMENT CALL, not a data gap: the 83x is an INTEGER-KEY INDEX-SPACE figure, so footprint_delta=0.012 applied to Dilmun's KV-store is a large cross-DOMAIN leap (bigger than the same-class cross-benchmark of the vetted entries) AND is composite-swinging (efficiency=1-footprint→~1.0). Options: (a) accept the modeled cross-domain mapping and vet it with the flag (consistent with how DNC/TARDIS/France/Brazil/Mexico were vetted 'modeled not proven'), or (b) require porting PLA-compression onto the actual Dilmun store first. Escalated to the user for the tournament-strictness decision rather than auto-promoting.

name: 🇲🇽 Alpha-Beta BAM
technique: Alpha-Beta Bidirectional Associative Memory: non-iterative one-step recall via binary Alpha/Beta order-operators + a one-hot orthonormal Linear-Associator stage, with a perfect-recall theorem and tolerance to a corrupted/variant cue
benchmark: Fingerprint↔number + English↔Spanish translator (CIC-IPN, Mexico; Computación y Sistemas 10(1), 2006)
metric: Sec.3: all 40 fingerprints + 120 EN→ES words 'perfectly recalled' incl. a misspelled-cue demo (Fig 8-9); runner-reported
maps_to: variant: recall the canonical stored item from a misspelled/incomplete cue
vetting: REJECTED, three independent reasons: (1) NATION+KEY COLLISION — Mexico is already occupied this session by 🇲🇽 EAM (key mx); re-keyed mxb only to preserve the record. (2) DOMINATED — on=1.0 on the variant axis folds via max() and 🇦🇪 root-key already saturates variant at 1.0, so it cannot move the loop (same class as 🇻🇳 Vietnam / 🇫🇮 Voikko). (3) QUALITATIVE — the runner itself notes variant-tolerance is a demonstrated typo-cue anecdote, not a swept recall-vs-edit-distance curve, so on=1.0 is the perfect-recall figure, not a measured variant-robustness number. Real deterministic mechanism (Acevedo-Mosqueda & Yanez-Marquez, CIC-IPN); re-admit only on a DIFFERENT open axis with a swept measured number. Kept on record so the runner skips re-proposing Alpha-Beta BAM.

name: 🇺🇦 context-dependent thinning
technique: Sparse binary distributed representations with Context-Dependent Thinning (Rachkovskij & Kussul): a deterministic conjunctive binding + thinning procedure forming fixed-density composite codes that support similarity-preserving superposition and structured recall
citation: Rachkovskij & Kussul, 'Binding and Normalization of Binary Sparse Distributed Representations by Context-Dependent Thinning', Neural Computation 13(2), 2001 (V.M. Glushkov Institute of Cybernetics, Kyiv) — landing pages 403/paywalled on fetch
benchmark: UNRESOLVED — sources 403'd / returned corrupted binary PDF on fetch
metric: NONE VERIFIED — no exact [0,1] number could be fetched
maps_to: robustness/retain: similarity-preserving recall of structured sparse codes
vetting: HELD PENDING — blocked on source, NOT rejected on merit. Mechanism and affiliation (Rachkovskij, V.M. Glushkov Institute of Cybernetics, Kyiv) are real and deterministic. UPDATE (this tick): made a second, expanded fetch pass — ALL routes blocked: MDPI (10.3390/info16050343) 403, Cogprints (cogprints.org/1240 + /537) TLS cert-mismatch + 403, Southampton web-archive mirror 403, doi.org redirect→MDPI 403, Semantic Scholar page returned empty body, MDPI /pdf endpoint 403. So still no quotable measured [0,1] number on a named benchmark. Separately note the original CDT paper reports an error-PROBABILITY analysis, not a clean benchmark accuracy, so even once a source opens it may not yield a loop-ready axis number. If admitted it would map to robustness (APPENDS to the mean, so it could move the loop). NOTE: citation is a bibliographic reference, not a fetched URL — do not treat as verified. Revisit only via a full browser (claude-in-chrome) or an open mirror that yields an exact figure; do not re-chase the routes already exhausted above.

name: 🇦🇹 modern Hopfield (DeepRC)
technique: Modern Hopfield network with continuous states and a deterministic one-step exponential-interaction retrieval rule (Ramsauer et al., JKU Linz)
metric: the only concrete number is a DeepRC immune-repertoire classification AUC — produced by a learned deep-architecture pipeline, not the deterministic retrieval rule in isolation
maps_to: robustness: associative retrieval from a partial cue
vetting: REJECTED — the deterministic modern-Hopfield update is real, but the paper's only concrete metric (immune-repertoire AUC) is generated by DeepRC, a LEARNED deep pipeline, so the number is not attributable to the deterministic memory rule alone. Same class as the 🇦🇺 Australia/MemoTime rejection (LLM/learned-pipeline metric). Re-admit only with a measured corrupted-cue retrieval accuracy attributable to the retrieval rule itself. Kept on record so the runner skips re-proposing JKU modern-Hopfield.
